pattern: Process cars right-to-left with a monotonic stack, discarding any car ahead that the current car can never catch before that car itself slows down.
hints: A car can only ever collide with the car immediately in front of it, but that car may have already merged into a fleet and slowed.
Work from the last car backward, keeping a stack of the cars still relevant ahead.
Pop a car ahead if it is faster (never caught) or if it would itself collide before the current car could reach it; the surviving top gives the collision time.

edge_inputs.description: all decreasing speeds, each catches the next
two cars, slower in front, collision happens

stress.description: many cars with strictly increasing positions and random speeds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    cars = []
    pos = rng.randrange(1, 5)
    for _ in range(n):
        speed = rng.randrange(1, 1000001)
        cars.append([pos, speed])
        pos += rng.randrange(1, 50)
    return [cars]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def getCollisionTimes(self, cars):
        n = len(cars)
        ans = [-1.0] * n
        stack = []
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            pi, si = cars[i]
            while stack:
                j = stack[-1]
                pj, sj = cars[j]
                if sj >= si:
                    stack.pop()
                    continue
                t = (pj - pi) / (si - sj)
                if ans[j] > 0 and t > ans[j]:
                    stack.pop()
                    continue
                ans[i] = t
                break
            stack.append(i)
        return ans
